item.title: Gut-reproduction axis: genome-wide pleiotropic and prospective evidence linking inflammatory bowel disease with gynecological disorders.
item.description: Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.] - We aimed to explore shared genetic architectures and potential causal associations between inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and gynecological diseases, including ovarian cysts (OC), pelvic inflamma...

item.title: The complement system in inflammatory bowel disease: from early observations to emerging frontiers.
item.description: Current opinion in gastroenterology - The complement system is one of the most evolutionarily conserved arms of innate immunity and has re-emerged as an important regulator of intestinal inflammation in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD)...

item.title: Cell-type-resolved genetic variation shapes inflammatory bowel disease risk.
item.description: Nature - Most genetic variants associated with complex diseases lie in non-coding regions1, complicating efforts to identify effector genes and relevant cell types. Here we map cis-expression quantitative t...

item.title: Whole genome-based polygenic risk score and structural variation analysis in Korean patients with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: BMB reports - The specific roles of single-nucleotide polymorphisms and structural variants (SVs) in Korean patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) susceptibility remain unclear; hence this study aimed to...

item.title: Exploring causal associations between autoimmune diseases and hearing loss: a mendelian randomization study.
item.description: Brazilian journal of otorhinolaryngology - The causal relationship between Autoimmune Diseases (ADs) and Hearing Loss (HL) remains unclear. This study investigates whether genetic predispositions associated with ADs contribute to HL risk. M...

item.title: IL23R genetic variants associate with Crohn's disease risk and microbiome changes in healthy first-degree relatives.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - Single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) in the Interleukin-23 receptor (IL23-R) gene are associated with Crohn's disease (CD), suggesting a role in pathogenesis and several biologic agents targeting...

item.title: Importance of vitamin D in gastrointestinal health and disease.
item.description: Frontiers in nutrition - Review - Since its discovery as an antirachitic agent, vitamin D has been recognized for its importance to health, most namely bone health, prompting food fortification practices that are still in place tod...
